explicit_text: Many of our drill instructors at boot camp had been on Guadalcanal, and they would tell us sea stories that would curl your hair. Hearing about their experiences made me eager to get my rifle and go fight. Guadalcanal was a tough battle that shaped the Marines who trained us.

explicit_text: We were one of the first platoons to get an M-1 rifle, which actually made us mad because we wanted the M1903 Springfield instead. The M-1 was new to us, and we envied the guys who had the '03 during boot camp. Later, as a rifle coach at Camp Mathews, I got really good with the M-1, even mastering off-hand shooting techniques.

Later, as a rifle coach at the marksmanship training center, I got really good with the semi-automatic, even mastering off-hand shooting techniques.

explicit_text: During boot camp, we envied the guys who had the M1903 Springfield rifle because it was considered the classic Marine rifle. explicit_text: After boot camp, I was sent to Camp Mathews because I was a good shot. I worked as a rifle range coach there for about six months, which was great duty since I was the boss and got to teach others to shoot well. It also improved my own marksmanship significantly.

explicit_text: I was stationed at Camp Pendleton after Camp Mathews, where one of the big things to do was stealing jeeps for fun. One time, after getting stuck in the mountains, we were caught and spent a tough weekend in the brig. It was also where the 4th Marine Division formed before we shipped out to the Pacific.

explicit_text: Joining the United States Marine Corps was a life-changing experience, especially boot camp, which was mentally and physically grueling. The Corps prepared us quickly for combat, with many veterans of Guadalcanal as drill instructors. I served with the 4th Marine Division and fought in major Pacific battles like Saipan and Tinian.

explicit_text: I was part of the 4th Marine Division, which formed at Camp Pendleton and went straight into combat in the Pacific. We made amphibious landings in the Marshall Islands, then trained hard on Maui before fighting at Saipan and Tinian. The division was well drilled and coordinated with the Navy for landing operations.

explicit_text: The Battle of Saipan was intense and brutal. We landed under heavy artillery fire and had to move quickly off the beach. After securing the island, we witnessed horrific mass suicides by Japanese soldiers and civilians at Marpi Point. Saipan was the first Japanese territory captured, and it was a turning point in the war.
